title: Fruity Indian Pineapple Curry
description: A sweet and spicy, Chettinad-inspired one‑pot curry featuring roasted pineapple chunks, deglazed with low‑sodium soy sauce and finished with coconut milk.
instructions: Heat oil in a deep skillet over medium heat and sauté onion until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and ginger, cooking until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Stir in diced red bell pepper and continue sautéing for 3 minutes.
Pour in tomato puree, low sodium soy sauce, ground cumin, coriander, and garam masala, stirring well.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and cook for 5 minutes to develop the base flavors.
Deglaze the pan with a splash of coconut milk, scraping up any browned bits.
Add pineapple chunks and half the coconut milk, mixing gently.
Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken and the pineapple to soften.
Stir in the remaining coconut milk and adjust salt if needed.
Finish with a handful of chopped fresh coriander leaves before serving.
